sections.title: Introduction and Background
sections.description: The corporation, financial statements, financial decision making, the time value of money, and interest rates (Berk & DeMarzo ch. 1–5 and 7).

sections.title: Valuing Bonds and Capital Budgeting
sections.description: Bond pricing, yield to maturity, the term structure of interest rates, and the fundamentals of investment decision rules (ch. 6 and 8).

sections.title: Valuing Stocks, CAPM and the Pricing of Risk
sections.description: Equity valuation models, capital markets, the historical risk–return trade-off, and the foundations of the Capital Asset Pricing Model (ch. 9–10).

sections.title: Optimal Portfolio Choice and the CAPM
sections.description: Portfolio theory, diversification, the efficient frontier, and the derivation and application of the CAPM (ch. 11).

sections.title: Cost of Capital and Market Efficiency
sections.description: Estimating the cost of equity and the weighted average cost of capital, plus the efficient markets hypothesis and its implications (ch. 12–13).

sections.title: Sustainable Finance
sections.description: ESG considerations in corporate finance, sustainability-linked investing and financing, and the role of climate and social risk in valuation.

sections.title: Capital Structure: Debt and Taxes
sections.description: The Modigliani–Miller propositions, the tax shield of debt, costs of financial distress, and how firms choose their financing mix (ch. 14–15).

sections.title: Payout Policy
sections.description: Dividends versus share repurchases, the payout policy irrelevance result, and the role of taxes, signalling and agency costs in payout decisions (ch. 17).

sections.title: Advanced Valuation
sections.description: Capital budgeting and valuation with leverage, including APV, WACC and FTE methods, and applying valuation to real corporate cases (ch. 18–19).

sections.title: Options
sections.description: Financial options, put–call parity, binomial and Black–Scholes pricing, and the role of options in corporate finance (ch. 20–22).

sections.title: Real Options
sections.description: Applying option-pricing logic to real investment decisions: timing, growth, abandonment and flexibility under uncertainty.

exam.format: Written exam (historically with both written-answer and multiple-choice components — see resources/_exams/)
